items.query: sweep the bug queue and drive everything to a mergeable state
items.notes: Primary phrase from description.

items.query: triage these bugs #123 #456 #789 and shepherd whatever already has a PR
items.notes: Explicit issue-list batch + shepherd verb.

items.query: shepherd in-flight community PRs through to merge this week
items.notes: Verbatim indirect trigger from description.

items.query: work down the bug backlog -- what's actually broken on main?
items.notes: Two verbatim indirect triggers combined.

items.query: batch-fix these issues: #221, #222, #223, #224
items.notes: Batch-fix verb on issue list.

items.query: process the bug backlog and dispatch fix sessions where no PR exists
items.notes: Process + dispatch + PR-detection language.

items.query: weekly sweep of community-reported bugs -- triage in parallel and report
items.notes: New indirect trigger added in v2.

items.query: shepherd all bug-flagged issues this quarter, do them in parallel
items.notes: New indirect trigger added in v2; distinguishes from single-PR review.

items.query: find legit bugs in the open issues and triage them in one pass
items.notes: New indirect trigger added in v2.

items.query: drive these in-flight PRs to merge: #1410, #1422, #1431
items.notes: Explicit in-flight PR list -- batch shepherding shape.

items.query: review my PR -- it's #1432, give me a full panel pass
items.notes: Single PR -> apm-review-panel directly, NOT batch-bug-shepherd.

items.query: panel-review this PR and post the recommendation
items.notes: Single-PR panel call -> apm-review-panel.

items.query: investigate one bug in detail and tell me the root cause
items.notes: Single-bug deep dive, NOT batch outer-loop.

items.query: draft a PR body for the auth refactor branch
items.notes: PR description work -> pr-description-skill.

items.query: open an issue describing the crash I just saw
items.notes: Issue creation, not batch triage.

items.query: write a release note for v0.13.0
items.notes: Release-note work.

items.query: explain this code path to me, I don't get the resolver flow
items.notes: Code explanation, not batch work.

items.query: update the docs site to mention the new copilot-app target
items.notes: Docs work, not bug shepherding.

items.query: add a flag to the CLI for verbose audit output
items.notes: Feature work, not bug-queue work.

items.query: design a new feature for marketplace versioning
items.notes: Design work, not batch triage.

items.query: fold the panel recommendations into PR #1234
items.notes: Single-PR fold-in -> apm-review-panel completion flow, NOT batch outer-loop. Guards against dispatch mis-fire after the fold-in invariant was added.

items.query: fold the followups into this one PR
items.notes: Single-PR phrasing using the new fold-in vocabulary; must not fire batch-bug-shepherd.

items.query: apply the panel recommendations to PR #998 and close it out
items.notes: Single-PR completion phrasing; remains apm-review-panel territory.

items.query: double-check none of the PRs we just marked ready went stale on main
items.notes: Mid-saga continuation of a bbs run; the new Phase 5 mergeability gate is the right home. The phrasing is plural ('the PRs') and references prior batch state ('we just marked ready'); routes to bbs.

items.query: rebase my branch onto main and resolve conflicts
items.notes: Single-branch git operation; routes to manual git, not bbs. Guards against the new 'rebase' vocabulary leaking out of the batch context.

items.query: this single PR has merge conflicts now, can you rebase it?
items.notes: Single-PR conflict resolution; routes to a one-off conflict-resolution session, not bbs outer loop.

items.query: sweep the bug queue but make sure we don't ship anything off-strategy
items.notes: Batch sweep with explicit strategic-alignment hint; routes to bbs Phase 1.5 gate.

items.query: triage open bugs and have the CEO persona check each against principles before we fix anything
items.notes: Names the Phase 1.5 mechanism (CEO persona vs PRINCIPLES.md) inside a batch context.

items.query: review my draft of PRINCIPLES.md and tell me if the rejection clauses are sharp enough
items.notes: Document-authoring on PRINCIPLES.md routes to doc-writer / apm-ceo direct, NOT bbs. Guards against the new 'PRINCIPLES.md' vocabulary leaking out of batch context.
